items.headline: City auditor reports on Silicon Valley Power's contract process
items.summary: The Audit Committee is reviewing findings from an audit of how Silicon Valley Power manages its power purchase agreements. This affects city energy costs and decisions about the municipally-owned utility.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program and increasing outreach to shopping centers, plus rolling out summer promotional campaigns to support local retail and small businesses. This could affect which businesses operate legally in Cupertino and may signal new city support for struggling commercial districts.

items.headline: Capitol Dental Office Opens on North Capitol Avenue
items.summary: A $500,000 dental office is opening at 585 N Capitol Av, bringing new healthcare services to the area. This represents significant commercial investment in San José's medical/professional services sector.

items.headline: City Council reviews federal housing grant applications
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission is reviewing applications for federal Community Development Block Grant funding on March 25, which supports affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. These grants can fund significant local housing initiatives.

items.headline: City updates zoning and building rules to boost retail businesses
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending its zoning and building codes to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan, aimed at supporting and revitalizing retail areas throughout the city. This could affect where new retail can be built and what regulations apply to storefronts and commercial spaces.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area planning study examines land use and transportation
items.summary: The Planning Commission is studying alternative land use and transportation scenarios for the San Antonio Road area. This exploratory study could shape future development and traffic patterns in that corridor.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: City Council gave second reading approval to a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road, moving the project forward with a development agreement. This project will add new housing to Mountain View.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, seeking state funding to support local housing development. This could bring grant money to accelerate housing projects in Mountain View.
